across.clue: to travel from place to place especially on foot without a particular direction or purpose.

To decide that something will not be done at the time when it was planned for but at a later time.
When an event or action is constant, it happens a lot or all of the time.
If something is necessary, you must do it.
Something fashionable is what people like to wear and do now.
If something is alongside another thing, then it is next to it.
To respond is to give an answer to what someone else said.
an organisation to which you give money so that it can give money and help to people who are poor or ill or who need advice and support

a legal agreement in which you borrow money from a bank in order to buy a house.
The subjects that students study at a particular school or college.

to describe something in a way that makes it seem better worse larger more important etc than it really is.
When something happens frequently, it happens often.

To affect the way someone thinks or behaves or affect the way something happens.
to collect a crop from the fields.

If something is incorrect, it is wrong.
